cost_components_summary: Annualised process conversion cost excluding explicitly listed commodity feedstocks, fuels and capture service; carbon prices excluded.
input_basis_notes: Direct material and energy inputs only. Auxiliary fluxes and minor alloying materials are omitted in Phase 1.
emissions_boundary_notes: Direct on-site route emissions only. Electricity-related emissions are excluded from end-use coefficients and should be represented through electricity-supply methods if desired.
rollout_limit_notes: Indicative upper-bound envelope only. Scrap availability, ore quality, hydrogen supply and plant replacement constraints are only partly represented.
availability_conditions: Conventional and scrap routes are available from 2025; CCS and H2 primary routes are treated as material only from 2030 onward through their share caps.
evidence_summary: Built from Australian steel-transition studies plus IEA route benchmarks and industry context, with explicit low-confidence caveats for project-specific future routes.
derivation_method: Route archetype synthesis. Per-tonne energy and process emissions are benchmarked to conventional, CCS-enabled, scrap-EAF and H2-DRI route literature, then smoothed across milestone years.
review_notes: Included because CCS remains a plausible transitional primary steel option, but Australian evidence is sparse and route economics are highly project-specific. In the decomposition pilot this row covers only the non-H2 DRI aggregate branch.
candidate_expansion_pathway: Retain as aggregate non-H2 crude-steel coverage while DRI production and DRI melt/refine are tested as explicit child roles.
times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes: This sector should ultimately expand to explicit process chains with material balances, capture modules, and possibly capacity vintages.
